content.text:
Adding `gcloud` to PATH in `.bashrc` so it persists across shells.

[REDACTED]

input.new_string:
PROMPT_COMMAND="${PROMPT_COMMAND:+$PROMPT_COMMAND; }precmd"
fi

# Google Cloud SDK
if [ -f "$HOME/google-cloud-sdk/google-cloud-sdk/path.bash.inc" ]; then
    . "$HOME/google-cloud-sdk/google-cloud-sdk/path.bash.inc"
fi
if [ -f "$HOME/google-cloud-sdk/google-cloud-sdk/completion.bash.inc" ]; then
    . "$HOME/google-cloud-sdk/google-cloud-sdk/completion.bash.inc"
fi

input.description: Confirm gcloud is on PATH after sourcing bashrc

content.text:
`gcloud` is installed and ready to use.

**Installed:** Google Cloud SDK 572.0.0 at `~/google-cloud-sdk/google-cloud-sdk/`

**PATH:** Added to `~/.bashrc` so new interactive shells pick it up automatically. In your current terminal, either open a new shell or run:

```bash
source ~/.bashrc
```

**Authenticate** (when you're ready to use GCP):

```bash
gcloud auth login
gcloud config set project YOUR_PROJECT_ID
```

For service account or application-default credentials:

```bash
gcloud auth application-default login
```

[REDACTED]

input.command: which aws 2>/dev/null || echo "not found"; aws --version 2>/dev/null || true
input.description: Check if AWS CLI is already installed

input.command: uname -m; which unzip curl; ls -la ~/.local/bin 2>/dev/null | head -5

cd /tmp && curl -fsSL "https://awscli.amazonaws.com/awscli-exe-linux-$(uname -m).zip" -o awscliv2.zip && unzip -q -o awscliv2.zip && ./aws/install -i "$HOME/.local/aws-cli" -b "$HOME/.local/bin" --update && rm -rf aws awscliv2.zip
input.description: Download and install AWS CLI v2 to user local

content.text:
AWS CLI v2 is installed.

**Installed:** `aws-cli/2.35.2` at `~/.local/bin/aws`

**PATH:** `~/.profile` already adds `~/.local/bin` to PATH for login shells. If `aws` isn’t found in your current terminal, run:

```bash
export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"
```

**Configure credentials** (when ready):

```bash
aws configure
```

Or use your existing `saml2aws` setup if that’s how you authenticate.

[REDACTED]
